+    def _resolve_dynamic_defaults(self, ctx: Context) -> None:
+        """Second-pass resolution for options whose ``default`` is a
+        mapping of other options' values to a default value. Runs after
+        every parameter has consumed its command line, environment
+        variable, and default map value, so the mapping can look at the
+        final values of the options it depends on.
+        """
+        params = self.get_params(ctx)
+        opt_to_param = {
+            opt: param for param in params for opt in (*param.opts, *param.secondary_opts)
+        }
+
+        for param in params:
+            dynamic_default = getattr(param, "dynamic_default", None)
+
+            if not dynamic_default:
+                continue
+
+            if ctx.get_parameter_source(param.name) != ParameterSource.DEFAULT:  # type: ignore[arg-type]
+                continue
+
+            resolved = None
+
+            for source_opt, mapping in dynamic_default.items():
+                source_param = opt_to_param.get(source_opt)
+
+                if source_param is None:
+                    continue
+
+                source_value = ctx.params.get(source_param.name)
+                key = str(source_value)
+
+                if key in mapping:
+                    resolved = mapping[key]
+
+            if resolved is not None:
+                ctx.params[param.name] = resolved  # type: ignore[index]
+
     def invoke(self, ctx: Context) -> t.Any:

+

+
